What it costs you

A "covered" claim
still costs thousands.

• Deductible — 2% of your dwelling coverage, not a flat fee. Resets on every claim.
• Depreciation — ACV policies pay only the aged value of your roof, not what replacement costs today.
• Code upgrades — if your home is older, you pay the difference to bring it to current code.
What your claim actually pays

Adjust the sliders to match your home. The math updates in real time.

Home insured value $400,000
$150k$900k
Roof age 10 years
1 yr25 yrs
Policy type ACV

Most Texas policies over 10 yrs old are ACV. Check your declarations page for "ACV" or "RCV" next to Coverage A.

Roof replacement cost (estimated) $22,000
Depreciation withheld (10 yrs × 4%/yr = 40%) −$8,800
Insurer's assessed value $13,200
Wind/hail deductible (2% of $400k) −$8,000
Insurance actually pays $5,200
You still owe the contractor $16,800
76% of total cost falls on you

Depreciation: 4%/yr (Travelers). Deductible: 2% dwelling (TDI). Replacement cost: $55/sq ft avg. ACV = depreciated value. RCV = full replacement.

What this costs if you stay in the cycle:

1 roof replacement (your out-of-pocket) $16,800
1× out of pocket
2× over 20 years + premium increases $36,000+
2× cycle

One time

Here's what actually happens.

You replace your roof.

It looks fine for a few years.

Then the next storm hits…

And you're right back where you started.

Same damage.
Same process.
Same bill.

Nothing actually changed.

So the real question isn't:

"Do you need a new roof?"

It's:

Why are you replacing
it at all?

The solution

This is not paint.

InsulShield is a nano-ceramic roof coating that bonds to your existing shingles and reinforces them against the forces that cause failure.

It helps the roof absorb and resist impact instead of breaking down under it.

No tear-off. No reset. No starting over.

2"
Hail — Written Warranty
Class 4
UL 2218 — Highest Rating
9/9
ASTM Tests Passed
15/30
Yr Warranty (w/ maintenance coat)

"9/9 ASTM tests" doesn't mean much until you see what those tests actually do to a roof. Each one below is an independent lab test — not self-reported. Click any test to see what it measured and watch the result.

UL
UL 2218 — Steel Ball Impact
Class 4 · Highest impact rating possible
PASSED

A 2-inch steel ball is dropped from 20 feet onto the coated shingle — simulating real hail impact at terminal velocity. Class 4 means zero cracking, zero penetration. Uncoated shingles routinely fail at Class 2.

UL 2218 IMPACT TEST Independent Lab · Class 4 Result
▶ Watch Test
Click to watch · Fullscreen available
IBHS
IBHS Ice Ball — 2" Hail Simulation
Real-world hail replication · 70+ mph
PASSED

Purpose-made 2-inch ice balls fired at the shingle surface at storm-speed — this is the test that directly maps to the written hail warranty. Zero surface damage on coated shingles. This is what "warranted against 2 inch hail" actually means in a lab.

IBHS HAIL IMPACT TEST Independent Lab · 2" Ice Ball Result
▶ Watch Test
Click to watch · Fullscreen available
D4977
ASTM D4977 — Granule Retention
+1,800% improvement over uncoated
PASSED

Granule loss is the #1 cause of shingle failure — it's what turns a 20-year roof into a 10-year roof. This test measures how much force is required to dislodge granules. InsulShield showed 18× better retention than bare shingles.

GRANULE RETENTION TEST ASTM D4977 · 18× Improvement
▶ Watch Test
Click to watch · Fullscreen available
D3161
ASTM D3161 — Wind Resistance
160 mph sustained · Cat-5 rated
PASSED

Shingles are subjected to 160 mph sustained wind in a lab wind tunnel — equivalent to a Category 5 hurricane. Not one coated shingle lifted, separated, or failed. Uncoated shingles at this speed commonly delaminate at the adhesive strip.

160 MPH WIND TEST ASTM D3161 · Cat-5 Rating
▶ Watch Test
Click to watch · Fullscreen available
E108
ASTM E108 — Fire Resistance
Class A rating — maximum protection
PASSED

Open flame applied directly to coated shingles. Class A is the highest fire resistance rating — the ceramic matrix resists ignition and slows flame spread. This matters most in wildland-urban interface zones and states with dry fire seasons.

CLASS A FIRE TEST ASTM E108 · Maximum Rating
▶ Watch Test
Click to watch · Fullscreen available

5 of 9 tests shown above · All 9 independently certified

Let's simplify it.

Most homeowners replace their roof every 10–15 years.

$18,000 – $35,000 each time.

Do that twice…

$36,000 – $70,000+ spent.

That's the cost of staying in the cycle.

Most people ask:

"How much does this cost?"

Wrong question.

How many times are you
willing to pay for your roof?

Common Questions

If this works…
why isn't everyone doing it?

If this works… why isn't everyone doing it?

Because the roofing industry is built around replacement, not prevention. Contractors get paid per job. Nobody profits from a roof that lasts 30 years.

This sounds like paint.

Paint is cosmetic. This is an engineered ceramic compound tested to UL 2218 Class 4 — the same impact standard required for the highest-rated impact-resistant shingles on the market.

What if my roof is too far gone?

Then this isn't for you — and we'll tell you that upfront. InsulShield only works on structurally sound shingles. If yours need replacement, we'll say so.

Will my insurance company care?

Some carriers discount premiums for Class 4 rated roofs. Ask your agent specifically about UL 2218 Class 4 documentation. Several homeowners have seen premium reviews after installation.
